title: Getting Real Work Done with Claude
goal: Use Claude day to day for real business tasks.

blocks.html: Claude is a strong writing and reasoning partner. It's great at long documents, careful drafts, and following detailed instructions — and it tends to tell you when it's unsure.

blocks.question: What is Claude especially good at?

options.text: Working through long, detailed writing and reasoning tasks carefully.
options.explain: Yes — long-form, careful work with clear instructions is its sweet spot.
Claude works with text, not images.
No AI guarantees facts. Always check the important ones yourself.

options.text: Running your whole business on autopilot.
options.explain: It's an assistant you direct, not a self-driving business.

blocks.html: The trick to Claude is a good, reusable setup. Build one you can paste again and again, then save it.

blocks.title: Reusable Claude prompt
blocks.template: You are helping me {job}. I'll give you {inputs}. Follow these rules: {rules}. Give me the result as {output}.

fields.label: Your rules
fields.placeholder: warm, under 5 sentences, no promises we can't keep

blocks.html: It's Monday: 40 unread emails, three to write, a report due. Pick how you'd use Claude to get through it.

start.text: How do you put Claude to work?

choices.label: Paste each thread and ask for a summary + draft reply, one at a time
Ask Claude to log into your email and handle it all
Write everything yourself and skip Claude

draft.text: You paste threads in and get summaries + draft replies.
draft.outcome: That's the workflow: Claude drafts, you approve and send. You stay in control and save hours.

login.text: You ask Claude to log into your email.
login.outcome: Claude can't log into your accounts — and you wouldn't want it to. You paste in what you want help with. Keep it draft-and-approve.

skip.text: You do it all by hand.
skip.outcome: You can — but that's the slow road. Even using Claude just for summaries and first drafts saves real time.

blocks.html: Same question, two ways to ask. Reveal why the setup matters more than the logo.

left.label: A vague quick chat
left.text: Fast, casual answers. Fine for a quick question, but it forgets your context and cuts corners on long work.

right.label: Claude, set up well
right.text: Give it a role, your context, and clear rules, and it does careful, long-form work that sounds like you.

why: How you prompt matters more than which tool — a clear role plus context beats a vague question every time.
Claude shines on longer, careful tasks: documents, detailed replies, structured reasoning.
For a 10-second fact, any chat tool is fine. For a client proposal, set it up properly.
Save your best setups as reusable prompts — you built one last lesson.
